title: erigeron allisonii
intro: Erigeron allisonii, also known as the Allison's fleabane, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and treating skin irritations. Erigeron allisonii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
